description_long: This is a slender, freestanding clock tower constructed primarily from dark wood, featuring a square base that tapers upward into a rectangular column. The top section houses a single, circular clock face with simple markings, set into a box-like enclosure. The roof of the tower is gently arched, with a decorative, gabled design that adds a touch of traditional elegance. The wood grain is visible throughout, giving the structure a natural, matte finish. The clock face is white with black hour and minute hands, and it lacks numerals, instead using simple tick marks for time indication. The overall design is minimalist yet classic, suitable for placement in parks, plazas, or along urban sidewalks.
description: A tall, dark wooden clock tower with a single white clock face and a gently arched, gabled roof. The design is minimalist and traditional, with visible wood grain and simple clock markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the narrow profile of the tower and the boxy clock enclosure, with no visible clock face.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the arched, gabled roof and the solid wooden back of the clock enclosure.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the narrow profile and the base structure.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the white circular clock face with black hands and tick marks, set into the wooden enclosure beneath the arched roof.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock face is circular and white, with simple black tick marks for each hour and no numerals. It features two black hands for hours and minutes, with no visible second hand or decorative elements.
